description.task: Implement a SimpleQueue class using a linked list of nodes. Support enqueue (add to rear), dequeue (remove from front), peek, isEmpty, and size. Demonstrate FIFO ordering.
instructions: First dequeue returns 10, second returns 20 (in order)
peek(), isEmpty(), size() are already implemented
description.hint: enqueue else: rear.next=n; rear=n. dequeue: front=front.next; if(front==null) rear=null;
description.expectedOutput:
Peek: 10
Size: 3
Dequeue: 10
Dequeue: 20
Size: 1
Peek: 30

description.task: Implement a QueueUsingStacks class that simulates a FIFO queue using two java.util.ArrayDeque instances acting as stacks. Enqueue always pushes to stack1; dequeue transfers all of stack1 to stack2 (if stack2 empty) then pops from stack2.
instructions: dequeue: if(stack2.isEmpty()) while(!stack1.isEmpty()) stack2.push(stack1.pop()); if(stack2.isEmpty()) throw RuntimeException; return stack2.pop();
Peek is already implemented — use same pattern as dequeue
FIFO: enqueue(1,2,3) then dequeue returns 1, enqueue(4), dequeue returns 2, then 3, then 4
The transfer reverses element order so stack2 serves them in FIFO order
description.hint: enqueue: stack1.push(val). dequeue: if(stack2.isEmpty()){while(!stack1.isEmpty()) stack2.push(stack1.pop());} if(stack2.isEmpty()) throw new RuntimeException('Empty'); return stack2.pop();

description.task: Implement a HotPotato class using a Queue to simulate the hot potato game. N players sit in a circle; the potato is passed k times per round; the player holding it after k passes is eliminated. Return the winner's name.
instructions: Eliminate front: queue.poll() (discard the returned value)
For players=[Alice,Bob,Carol,Dave,Eve] and k=2: winner is Carol
For k=3: winner is Eve (Josephus variant)
